summary: Small multi-file scratch implementation touching camera, Flat Zone ground, DevText text drawing/text library, and baselib particle code. The diff mainly adjusts local expression shapes and matching tactics: pointer temporaries in gmCamera, scoped dont_inline pragmas and PAD_STACK in DevText, an indexed array loop in particle cleanup, and minor control-flow/local declaration reshaping in grflatzone.

key_files.role: Adjusted gmCamera_801A30E4 to use a gcus local, pointer temporaries for xC and x8, and alternate JObj access expressions.
Reshaped grFlatzone_80218260 by hoisting HSD_JObj* declaration and combining GET_JOBJ assignment with a NULL check.
Added scoped dont_inline pragmas around DevText_InitPool and DevText_CreateCObj, added PAD_STACK(8) in DevText_Setup, and masked render priority to 8 bits.
Added scoped dont_inline pragmas around several small DevText flag/erase helpers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:textlib.c DevText_ShowBackground, DevText_HideBackground, DevText_ShowText, DevText_HideText, DevText_Erase hunks

key_files.role: Simplified hsd_8039CEAC by removing a bins pointer local and indexing hsd_804D0908 directly inside the loop.

what_changed: PR body says it resolves issues #2550 through #2554, but the slice does not include those issue bodies.
gmCamera_801A30E4 now caches gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus in a local pointer and uses pointer locals for gcus->xC and gcus->x8 in repeated accesses.
grFlatzone_80218260 changed a nested block-local GET_JOBJ pattern into an assignment expression inside the NULL check.
DevText functions gained tightly scoped #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop blocks around InitPool, CreateCObj, ShowBackground, HideBackground, ShowText, HideText, and Erase.
DevText_Setup gained PAD_STACK(8) and passes devtext_setup_render_priority & 0xFF to GObj_SetupGXLink.
hsd_8039CEAC now indexes hsd_804D0908[i] directly instead of walking a void** bins pointer.

smart_moves.text: Scoped each dont_inline pragma with push/pop rather than leaving a broad pragma state in effect.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:textlib.c DevText flag helper and Erase hunks

smart_moves.text: Kept the particle loop as a natural for-loop over 16 entries while changing the access form from pointer increment to array indexing.
Reduced repeated long global/member expressions in gmCamera_801A30E4 by introducing gcus and pointer locals.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For scratch or matching work, small expression-shape changes can be spread across unrelated files; preserve each file's local evidence rather than inferring one broad subsystem rule.
evidence_refs: changed_files: five changed files across gm, gr, if, and sysdolphin

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a loop already has a clear counter, direct array indexing may be a viable alternative to carrying a separate incremented pointer local.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:particle.c removed bins pointer and bins++ while using hsd_804D0908[i]

naming_conventions.name: px8
evidence_refs: raw_diff:gmcamera.c added HSD_JObj** px8 and px8 = &gcus->x8

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use scoped #pragma dont_inline blocks around small helpers to affect inlining while limiting pragma scope.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review comment or objdiff evidence is included explaining why dont_inline was required; current decomp standards treat pragmas as exceptions needing tight evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK(8) in DevText_Setup to affect stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice includes only an automated report saying no changes, not a narrow objdiff rationale for PAD_STACK.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Force an 8-bit value at a callsite by passing devtext_setup_render_priority & 0xFF.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:textdraw.c changed GObj_SetupGXLink argument to devtext_setup_render_priority & 0xFF

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce pointer-to-field locals to influence repeated global/member access codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:gmcamera.c added pxc and px8 and used *pxc, *px8

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use an assignment expression in a NULL check for a local JObj.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 states: No changes.
No human review comments or review records are present in the loaded slice.

follow_up_queries: decomp-dev bot GALE01 No changes PR 2560

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed file counts, merge status
human_pr_text: PR body with resolved issue checklist
raw_diff: full diff for five files
notes: The diff and metadata are complete enough to identify changed files and local code tactics.
The PR body is minimal and only lists resolved issues; issue contents are not loaded, so the meaning of 'scratches' and each resolved issue cannot be reconstructed from this slice.
No human review comments are loaded, so reviewer intent and rationale are unknown.
The automated GALE01 report says 'No changes', but the slice does not include detailed objdiff/build output per symbol; matching claims should remain cautious.
Pragmas and PAD_STACK are visible tactics, but the PR evidence does not explain why they were necessary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2560 used tightly scoped dont_inline pragmas in DevText scratch work
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2560, several DevText helpers were wrapped with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop. This is evidence of a historical matching tactic, not proof that dont_inline is generally preferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2560 replaced particle bin pointer walking with direct indexed access
accepted_candidate_records.text: hsd_8039CEAC removed a bins pointer local and bins++ increment, using hsd_804D0908[i] directly inside the existing 16-iteration loop.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR 2560 as a cautionary example for scoped pragma and PAD_STACK tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2560 contains scoped dont_inline pragmas and PAD_STACK(8) in DevText scratch work. If curated, it should be documented only as a historical matching tactic that still needs narrow objdiff/build evidence, aligning with existing standards that pragmas and stack padding are exceptions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:textdraw.c added #pragma dont_inline blocks and PAD_STACK(8)
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report says No changes
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR gives a compact historical example of tightly scoped pragmas and stack padding, but lacks detailed verifier rationale; route for curator review rather than promoting as accepted style.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider cross-reference from natural loop guidance to direct array indexing examples
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2560's hsd_8039CEAC keeps a 16-iteration for-loop and replaces a walked pointer local with hsd_804D0908[i]. This may be useful as an example that natural loop structure can coexist with direct indexed global-array access when it matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:particle.c removed bins local and bins++
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reusable example; not enough evidence to make a stronger rule without object-level matching detail.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all DevText helpers should be dont_inline; this PR only shows specific scoped additions.
Do not infer accepted semantics for gcus, pxc, or px8 beyond their immediate field-pointer roles in the diff.
Do not claim the five resolved issues' contents or validation criteria; the issue bodies are not included in the slice.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(8) or comma-expression access in gmCamera as normal style without additional objdiff/build evidence.
